latest.title: foxes hunt in cities and we almost miss it
latest.script_key: city foxes don t just scavenge they hunt on quiet streets because rodents hide in warm storm drains that s why you ll see their ears twitch toward manhole covers look for their paws barely making noise foxes step like cats did you know they use headlights to spot prey

latest.title: Cats sunbathe for a surprising reason
latest.script_key: cats don t sunbathe for warmth that s why they often stretch sideways exposing their belly and spine to direct sunlight watch this one twice their fur traps heat but their skin gets hit fastest where blood vessels run closest did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats reveal the grooming signal first watch closely because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Rock layers tell mountains secrets
latest.script_key: watch the stripes climb those dark streaks aren t dirt they re ancient mud cracked open and refilled with new minerals that s why they stand out every layer is a climate snapshot from millions of years ago did you learn in school that mountains only grow upward they shrink too

latest.title: Rivers carve bends by stealing from one bank
latest.script_key: rivers reveal the river bend first watch closely because faster water cuts that bank while slower water drops sediment inside the curve over time the whole river shifts across the landscape a bend is motion written in mud which water shape next

latest.title: Rivers sort mud and sand along each bank
latest.script_key: rivers sort sediment while they bend watch the edge of the flow because slower water drops heavier material while faster water keeps cutting that sorting is how a quiet bend slowly redraws the map which river edge should we compare next

latest.title: Ants carry rocks bigger than their heads
latest.script_key: they carry rocks bigger than themselves look how they grip the edge that s why they don t just walk around they engineer ants don t lift with their backs they lock jaws and pull like tiny bulldozers would you survive a day under that rock pile

latest.title: lemurs use tails like fifth limbs
latest.script_key: tails aren t just cute notice how that tail curls around the branch like a finger that s exactly what it is a fifth limb lemurs use them like hands to balance while they lunge for food that s why they can sprint along twigs no bigger than pencils would you trust a tail to catch you on that

latest.title: Forests can cool the air below them
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next
